methods.description: Process of studying and logging outcrops and boreholes to establish a new lithostratigraphy for the onshore Danish Miocene succession.

steps.description: During numerous field campaigns in the past 20 years, 25 outcrops have been studied and logged by conventional facies analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field campaigns to study and log outcrops using facies analysis

steps.description: A total of 50 boreholes across Jylland have been described for sediment texture.

methods.description: Palynological analysis of sediment samples to study dinoflagellate cyst assemblages and establish a chronostratigraphic framework.

steps.description: The sediment samples were processed using standard palynological preparation methods, including treatment with HCl to dissolve calcareous matter and with HF to dissolve silicates.
steps.searchable_summary: process sediment samples with HCl and HF for palynological analysis

steps.description: The acid treatment was followed by neutralisation with water, brief oxidation with KOH and sieving on 20 µm nylon mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: neutralize and oxidize samples, then sieve on 20 µm mesh

steps.description: The organic residues, including the dinocysts, were then mounted on a glass slide using glycerin gel and studied using a normal light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: mount organic residues on glass slide and study with microscope
